title: USD Schema Namespace Source Contract

included: wrapping a composed namespace source with schema-derived prim definitions
using composed typeName and composed apiSchemas field data as schema inputs
fallbackPrimTypes substitution when the authored typeName is not registered and a registered fallback type is available
typed schema inheritance into the prim definition
multiple applied schema instance-name property injection
schema inclusions from registered built-ins and auto-applies
multiple applied schema inclusion instance propagation
override properties that sparsely strengthen included schema property fields
diagnostics for schema inclusions or override properties that violate the registry-backed schema contract
schema-defined property field maps containing typeName, custom, variability, and fallback default fields when supplied by the registry
authored composed property specs overriding schema-defined property fields
property child enumeration merging authored properties with schema-defined prim-definition properties
strongest propertyOrder applied to the authored-plus-schema property set
deferred: schema definition discovery from files, plugins, or process-global registries
schema family version negotiation beyond exact registered names
domain algorithms for specific schemas such as collections, color, xforms, materials, or geometry
attribute value interpolation or value-clip evaluation

model.SchemaNamespaceSource: A namespace-source wrapper that injects schema-defined property inputs and prim-definition context without evaluating composition arcs itself.
model.PrimDefinition: Per-prim schema context built from effective typed schema, applied schemas, and registered schema property definitions.
model.SchemaPropertyInput: A property input whose field map originates from a schema definition and is weaker than an authored property input at the same path.

output.mode: namespace source mode inherited from the wrapped source
output.composition: composition level inherited from the wrapped source
output.value_resolution: value-resolution level inherited from the wrapped source
output.child_ordering: child ordering inherited from the wrapped source

output.exists: whether the prim exists in the wrapped source
output.authoredType: composed authored typeName token when present
output.effectiveType: registered concrete typed schema used for the prim definition, after fallbackPrimTypes substitution if applicable
output.typeless: true when no concrete registered type applies
output.appliedSchemas: ordered applied schema tokens from apiSchemas
output.schemaOrder: strong-to-weak schema order used for fallback acquisition
output.properties: schema-defined property names contributed by the prim definition

output.fields: source-visible prim fields from the wrapped source
output.childNames: candidate child prim names from the wrapped source
output.propertyNames: authored and schema-defined property names in composed order

output.exists: whether an authored or schema-defined property input exists
output.fields: authored fields merged over weaker schema-defined fields
output.schemaDefined: whether the property exists in the prim definition
output.authored: whether the wrapped source supplies an authored/composed property input

output.diagnostics: diagnostics from the wrapped source plus schema-source diagnostics

laws: the schema source must consume a namespace source and must not read parser-local USDA structures
the schema source must not evaluate references, payloads, inherits, specializes, variants, relocates, or layer stacks locally
schema definition ingestion is implementation-defined by AOUSD v1.0.1 and must be supplied explicitly at this boundary

the composed prim definition uses a concrete registered typed schema when the composed typeName names one; abstract or unregistered type names leave the prim typeless unless fallbackPrimTypes supplies a registered concrete substitute
apiSchemas is interpreted on the composed prim, not at the layer level
multiple applied schema property names are formed by substituting the recorded instance name into the schema definition's placeholder property name
schema built-ins and auto-applies are registry-supplied inclusions in the owning schema's prim definition and are composed as weaker schema definitions

a multiple applied schema included by type from another multiple applied schema uses the owning schema's recorded instance name; a named multiple applied built-in under another multiple applied schema prefixes the owning instance name to the included instance name
override properties sparsely strengthen metadata fields on included properties, do not define new properties, and must not override typeName or variability
schema-defined properties participate in property child enumeration before propertyOrder is applied
authored composed property fields are stronger than schema-defined property fields for the same property path
schema fallback defaults remain schema-defined fallback data for value resolution and must not be reclassified as authored defaults
stage population may consume schema-defined property names from this source, but must not compute prim definitions itself

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing rootLayer, optional sublayers/referenceLayers, a schemaRegistry object, and ops. Schema definitions are a conformance input for the implementation-defined schema registry boundary.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and operation results.
